engineIntelligence.h2: Chrysler Aspen Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Chrysler Aspen in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data. Every unit is sourced to ensure perfect ECU and transmission compatibility for your imported SUV.

cards.altText: Chrysler Aspen 5.7 HEMI engine - petrol 5.7L replacement UK
knownFailures: Hemi tick - Exhaust manifold bolts fracture, causing a loud tapping noise and exhaust leaks.
MDS collapse - Multi-Displacement System lifters fail, leading to camshaft damage.

cards.altText: Chrysler Aspen 4.7 PowerTech engine - petrol 4.7L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing chain stretch - Plastic guides wear prematurely, causing rattling on startup.
Oil consumption - Worn piston rings and valve seals lead to excessive oil burning.

cards.altText: Chrysler Aspen 5.7 HEMI engine - petrol 5.7L replacement UK
knownFailures: Overheating and warped heads - Cooling system neglect leads to catastrophic cylinder head damage.
Oil pump failure - Low oil pressure causes rapid bottom-end bearing wear and engine knock.

engineGuide.h2: Chrysler Aspen Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Chrysler Aspen across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 4.7L Petrol
entries.history: The standard naturally aspirated V8 for early Aspen models, offering reliable low-end torque but prone to timing chain wear.
compatibleVariants: Base, Base AWD, Base RWD, Limited, Limited AWD, Limited RWD
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch and plastic guide failure around 80k-100k miles.
Excessive oil consumption due to worn piston rings.
entries.cta: Get quotes for EVC Chrysler Aspen engine replacement

entries.title: 4.7L Petrol (Flex-Fuel)
entries.history: An updated variant of the 4.7L capable of running on E85 ethanol, featuring revised ECU mapping and sensor arrays.
commonFailures: Flex-fuel sensor degradation causing poor fuel trim and misfires.
Timing chain tensioner wear similar to the EVC variant.
entries.cta: Get quotes for EVF Chrysler Aspen engine replacement

entries.title: 5.7L Petrol
entries.history: The foundational HEMI V8 for the Aspen, introducing Multi-Displacement System (MDS) for improved fuel economy, but with known lifter vulnerabilities.
compatibleVariants: Base, Base AWD, Base RWD, Limited, Limited AWD, Limited RWD
commonFailures: "Hemi tick" from fractured exhaust manifold bolts.
MDS lifter collapse leading to camshaft lobe wear.
entries.cta: Get quotes for EZB Chrysler Aspen engine replacement

entries.title: 5.7L Petrol
entries.history: A mid-production refinement of the 5.7L HEMI, featuring minor updates to the valvetrain and emissions systems to address early EZB complaints.
commonFailures: Persistent MDS solenoid and lifter failures if oil changes are neglected.
Oil pump pickup tube screen clogging causing low oil pressure.
entries.cta: Get quotes for EZD Chrysler Aspen engine replacement

entries.title: 5.7L Petrol
entries.history: The final and most refined iteration of the 5.7L HEMI for the Aspen, benefiting from all previous running changes and improved durability.
commonFailures: Exhaust manifold bolt failure (though less frequent than EZB).
entries.cta: Get quotes for EZH Chrysler Aspen engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Chrysler Aspen Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Chrysler Aspen’s V8 engines are robust but carry specific, well-documented vulnerabilities as they age. The 5.7L HEMI’s MDS lifter collapse and exhaust manifold "tick", alongside the 4.7L PowerTech’s timing chain stretch, dominate UK replacement demand. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address these original failure points - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this guide to weigh your repair versus replacement options.

problems.h4: HEMI "Tick" & Exhaust Manifold Bolt Failure - Why Chrysler Aspen Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Chrysler Aspen Base, Limited, AWD, RWD (2007-2009, EZB, EZD, EZH)
problems.rootCause: The factory exhaust manifold bolts are prone to snapping due to thermal cycling and stress, causing an exhaust leak that mimics a valvetrain "tick".

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Removing the manifold and replacing snapped studs and bolts.
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if caught early before warping the manifold.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Swapping the entire unit with a rebuilt engine featuring upgraded manifold hardware.
repairOptions.longevity: Best long-term solution if internal damage has occurred.

problems.recommendation: If the manifold is not warped, a specialist stud extraction and bolt replacement is highly cost-effective. However, if the "tick" is accompanied by oil consumption or camshaft noise, budget for a full rebuilt engine to prevent catastrophic failure.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 5.7L HEMI engine prices for your Chrysler Aspen

problems.h4: MDS (Multi-Displacement System) Lifter Collapse - Why Chrysler Aspen Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Chrysler Aspen 5.7L HEMI variants (2007-2009, EZB, EZD, EZH)
problems.rootCause: The MDS lifters, which deactivate cylinders to save fuel, can stick or collapse due to poor oil pressure or degraded oil, subsequently destroying the camshaft lobes.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Removing cylinder heads to replace collapsed lifters and the damaged camshaft.
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if the block and bearings are undamaged.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Installing a fully rebuilt engine with upgraded, reinforced MDS components.
repairOptions.longevity: The most reliable path for high-mileage units to avoid repeat failures.

problems.recommendation: MDS failure rarely stays isolated. Once a lifter collapses, metal debris circulates through the engine. We strongly recommend a full rebuilt engine over a partial top-end repair for any Aspen with over 80,000 miles.
problems.cta: Get quotes for MDS-failure proof Chrysler Aspen engine replacement

problems.h4: 4.7L PowerTech Timing Chain Stretch - Why Chrysler Aspen Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.rootCause: The plastic timing chain guides degrade over time, allowing the chain to stretch and slap, which can lead to jumped timing and catastrophic valve-to-piston contact.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacing the chains, guides, and tensioners before failure occurs.
repairOptions.longevity: Highly recommended as preventative maintenance around 70k miles.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fitting a reconditioned 4.7L engine with updated timing components.
repairOptions.longevity: Necessary if the chain has already jumped and caused internal damage.

problems.recommendation: If you hear a rattling noise on cold startup, act immediately. A preventative timing kit replacement is far cheaper than a full engine replacement, but if the engine has already suffered interference damage, a rebuilt unit is your safest investment.
problems.cta: Find preventative and rebuilt 4.7L V8 engine options for your Aspen

problems.h4: Excessive Oil Consumption & Bottom-End Knock - Why Chrysler Aspen Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.rootCause: Worn piston rings and valve stem seals allow oil to enter the combustion chamber, leading to low oil levels, bearing starvation, and eventual rod knock.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacing valve seals, piston rings, and bearings without removing the engine.
repairOptions.longevity: Only a temporary fix if the cylinder walls are scored.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: A complete machined rebuild with new pistons, rings, and bearings.
repairOptions.longevity: The only permanent solution for an engine exhibiting rod knock.

problems.recommendation: Once an engine develops a deep knocking sound or burns more than a litre of oil per 1,000 miles, the block is likely compromised. Do not waste money on partial repairs; opt for a fully rebuilt engine with a warranty.
problems.cta: Compare prices for a fully rebuilt Chrysler Aspen V8 engine today

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Chrysler Aspen
finalCta.paragraph: Ignoring early warning signs like a Hemi tick, rattling timing chain, or oil consumption can turn a manageable repair into a total engine loss. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Chrysler Aspen specialists, ensuring you get a fair deal. All our network suppliers provide upgraded components to fix known factory flaws, nationwide delivery, and a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Compare prices and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Chrysler Aspen Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket’s UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Chrysler Aspen and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Chrysler Aspen Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Chrysler Aspen is listed below, complete with compatible engine codes, production years, and common failure notes to help you identify the correct replacement.

items.title: Chrysler Aspen 4.7L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 4.7L PowerTech V8 served as the entry-level and standard powerplant for the Chrysler Aspen during its short UK import run. While it offers adequate low-end torque for the heavy SUV chassis, it is primarily known for timing chain guide degradation and oil consumption issues as mileage accumulates.
items.cta: Get quotes for Chrysler Aspen 4.7L petrol engines

items.title: Chrysler Aspen 5.7L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 5.7L HEMI V8 is the dominant and most sought-after engine for the Chrysler Aspen, offering robust performance and towing capability. However, its Multi-Displacement System (MDS) and fragile exhaust manifold bolts make it susceptible to the notorious "Hemi tick" and lifter collapse if maintenance is neglected.
commonFailurePoints: Fractured exhaust manifold bolts causing a "tick"
MDS lifter collapse leading to camshaft damage.
items.cta: Compare Chrysler Aspen 5.7L petrol engine prices

engineYears.h2: Chrysler Aspen Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: Use this Chrysler Aspen year guide to match your vehicle to the right engine family, production period, and replacement options.

years.description: The first year of the Chrysler Aspen introduced the platform to the UK as a rare, left-hand-drive import, featuring the foundational 4.7L and 5.7L V8 engines.
keyChanges: Introduction of the Chrysler Aspen to the UK market as a niche luxury SUV import.
Standard fitment of the 4.7L EVC and 5.7L EZB HEMI engines.
Establishment of the Base and Limited trim levels with RWD and AWD options.
knownFor: Raw, unrefined American V8 character with basic interior tech.
Early EZB HEMI engines exhibiting the first wave of exhaust manifold bolt fractures.
High import rarity means parts can sometimes require cross-referencing with the Dodge Durango.
years.cta: Get quotes for 2007 Chrysler Aspen replacement engines
years.ticker: 2007 Chrysler Aspen 4.7 V8 engine replacement • 2007 Chrysler Aspen 5.7 HEMI MDS failure • 2007 Aspen engine supply and fit

years.description: The 2008 model year brought subtle but crucial updates to the valvetrain and introduced the flex-fuel capable EVF engine code to the lineup.
keyChanges: Introduction of the EVF 4.7L Flex-Fuel variant for select markets.
Transition to the EZD 5.7L HEMI code, featuring minor valvetrain refinements.
Updated ECU calibrations to improve emissions and fuel economy.
knownFor: Slightly improved reliability over the 2007 models due to running changes.
Continued vulnerability to MDS lifter collapse if oil change intervals are extended.
High search volume for "2008 Chrysler Aspen 5.7 Hemi engine replacement".
years.cta: Compare prices for 2008 Chrysler Aspen engine replacements
years.ticker: 2008 Chrysler Aspen 5.7 Hemi EZD replacement • 2008 Aspen 4.7 V8 timing chain fix

years.description: The final year of Chrysler Aspen production featured the most refined iterations of both V8 engines, codenamed EVF and EZH, before the model was discontinued.
keyChanges: Rollout of the EZH 5.7L HEMI code, representing the most durable iteration of the engine.
Final refinement of the 4.7L EVF engine with updated sensor arrays.
End of production for the Chrysler Aspen, cementing its status as a rare UK import.
knownFor: Best overall reliability of the Aspen lineup due to final-year manufacturing tweaks.
EZH engines still require vigilance regarding exhaust manifold bolts, though less prone to failure than EZB.
High desirability for low-mileage, rebuilt examples to keep these rare SUVs on the road.

faq.h2: Chrysler Aspen Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Chrysler Aspen engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A Chrysler Aspen engine replacement typically costs between £1,500 and £3,500 for a rebuilt unit, depending on whether you have the 4.7L or 5.7L V8. Used options start around £800, while full supply and fit packages will add £600-£1,200 in labour.
keyPoints: 4.7L rebuilt engines average £1,500-£2,800.
5.7L HEMI rebuilt engines average £1,800-£3,500.
Supply and fit labour adds £600-£1,200.
All rebuilt units include a 12-month warranty.
items.cta: Compare Chrysler Aspen engine replacement prices today

items.question: Can I put a Dodge Durango engine in a Chrysler Aspen?
items.answer: Yes, the Chrysler Aspen is essentially a rebadged Dodge Durango, meaning cross-compatibility is excellent. As long as the engine code (e.g., EZB, EVC) and model year match, a Durango engine will fit perfectly.
keyPoints: Aspen and Durango share the same platform and mounting points.
Engine codes must match exactly for ECU compatibility.
Wiring harnesses and sensor arrays are interchangeable.
Always verify the VIN before purchasing a cross-platform unit.
items.cta: Get a Chrysler Aspen engine compatibility check now

items.question: What are the signs of Chrysler Aspen engine failure?
items.answer: Key warning signs include a loud "Hemi tick" from the exhaust, rattling on cold startup, excessive oil consumption, and illuminated check engine lights for misfires or low oil pressure.
keyPoints: Hemi tick indicates fractured exhaust manifold bolts.
Cold startup rattling points to 4.7L timing chain stretch.
Blue exhaust smoke signals severe oil burning.
MDS lifter collapse often causes a sudden, deep engine knock.
items.cta: Find a reliable replacement engine for your Chrysler Aspen

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in a Chrysler Aspen?
items.answer: Yes, replacing the engine is highly viable and often more economical than purchasing another vehicle, given the Aspen's robust chassis and the availability of affordable, warrantied reconditioned V8 units.
keyPoints: A rebuilt engine costs significantly less than a new SUV.
The Aspen's chassis and drivetrain are durable and long-lasting.
Reconditioned units fix factory flaws like MDS weaknesses.
Extends the vehicle's life by another 100,000+ miles.
items.cta: Request a Chrysler Aspen rebuilt engine quote today

items.question: How long does a rebuilt Chrysler Aspen engine last?
items.answer: A professionally rebuilt Chrysler Aspen engine can easily exceed 100,000 miles when paired with strict adherence to service intervals, especially using high-quality synthetic oil to protect the MDS system.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines replace all critical wear items.
Upgraded timing components prevent early failure.
Regular 5,000-mile oil changes are critical for HEMI longevity.
Backed by a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty.
items.cta: View our reconditioned Chrysler Aspen engine options

items.question: Does a Chrysler Aspen engine replacement include a warranty?
items.answer: Yes, every engine sourced through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Supply and fit packages often include comprehensive parts and labour coverage.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month warranty on all rebuilt units.
Unlimited mileage coverage for total peace of mind.
Supply and fit includes labour warranty.
Warranty terms are clearly stated by each UK specialist.
items.cta: Get quotes for Chrysler Aspen engine replacement with warranty

items.question: What is the difference between rebuilt and reconditioned Chrysler Aspen engines?
items.answer: While often used interchangeably, "reconditioned" typically implies a more thorough restoration to exact factory tolerances, whereas "rebuilt" may only replace the specifically failed components and adjacent wear items.
keyPoints: Reconditioned engines are fully stripped and machined.
Rebuilt engines target specific failure points (e.g., lifters).
Both options are vastly superior to untested used engines.
Reconditioned units command a slight premium but offer maximum longevity.
items.cta: Compare rebuilt vs reconditioned Chrysler Aspen engine prices

items.question: How long does Chrysler Aspen engine supply and fit take?
items.answer: A typical Chrysler Aspen engine supply and fit process takes 3 to 7 working days, covering sourcing, nationwide delivery, professional installation, and final ECU calibration and road testing.
keyPoints: Sourcing a matched engine takes 1-3 days.
Nationwide delivery adds 1-2 days.
Professional installation requires 1-2 days.
Includes post-fit diagnostics and MDS system calibration.
items.cta: Book Chrysler Aspen engine supply and fit today

items.question: What causes the 5.7L HEMI MDS failure in the Chrysler Aspen?
items.answer: MDS (Multi-Displacement System) failure is primarily caused by degraded oil or extended service intervals, leading to lifter collapse, which subsequently destroys the camshaft lobes and can send metal debris through the engine.
keyPoints: MDS lifters deactivate cylinders to save fuel.
Poor oil pressure causes these lifters to stick or collapse.
Collapsed lifters grind against the camshaft, causing severe damage.
Upgraded lifters or MDS delete kits are common rebuild solutions.
items.cta: Compare prices for MDS-proof rebuilt 5.7L HEMI engines

items.question: Can I get a remanufactured 5.7 HEMI for my Chrysler Aspen?
items.answer: Yes, while rare, we can source or commission remanufactured 5.7L HEMI units for customers demanding absolute, zero-mile reliability that exceeds original factory specifications.
keyPoints: Remanufactured engines are blueprinted to exact tolerances.
All internal components are brand new or freshly machined.
Ideal for owners planning to keep their Aspen indefinitely.
Availability is limited, so early enquiry is recommended.
